front: Play: arrogant soldier Pyrgopolynices ('Conqueror of Many Towers') with comically large shield carried by servants
Play: slave Palaestrio invents character 'Honoria' using a hole in a partition wall to trick the braggart soldier
Play: helps Pleusicles rescue Philocomasium; courtesan Acroteleutium pretends to love Pyrgopolynices
Play: flatterer Artotrogus constantly praises the title soldier (parasitus stock character)
Play: inspired commedia dell'arte's Il Capitano
Play: separated twins cause confusion with prostitute Erotium and dogsbody Peniculus in Epidamnus
Play: inspired Shakespeare's Comedy of Errors
Play: wife's mantle mistakenly given to prostitute Erotium
Play: Sosicles changes name after twin kidnapped by man from Epidamnus
Play: miser Euclio guards the title treasure; kills a rooster scratching near it
Play: Lyconides receives the gold as dowry; basis for Moliere's The Miser
Play: clever slave rescues Calidorus's lover Phoenicium from the pimp Ballio by impersonating messenger Harpax; 2,000 drachma bets
Play: loosely adapted by Sondheim's A Funny Thing Happened on the Way to the Forum
Play: contains first reference to 'tragicomedy'; only Plautus play on a mythological subject
Play: Zeus seduces Alcumena disguised as her husband; Mercury impersonates servant Sosia (French 'sosie' = look-alike)
Play: basis for Moliere's version, later adapted by Dryden and Heinrich von Kleist
Play: star Arcturus foretells shipwreck of wicked pimp Labrax; Daemones discovers his daughter Palaestra via childhood toys in a chest
Playwright: plays' completeness varies by alphabet because the monk scrubbing the Ambrosian palimpsest got tired midway through
Playwright: oldest extant source for the Latin saying 'homo homini lupus' ('man is wolf to man')
Playwright: Poenulus is the only extant Latin text preserving Carthaginian Punic; Milphio 'translates' Hanno's words into nonsense about 'African mice'
Playwright: stock character senex amator (lustful old man) exemplified by Lysidamus in Casina
Playwright: Machiavelli's Clizia based on a lost play by this Roman comic dramatist
